rag_rules: Repair only validator-reported scope or type mismatches in AGREE annexes without altering AADL structure.
Preserve all existing component types, connections, and owners while fixing invalid references.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers visible in the AADL model or declared locally within the annex.
Align helper declarations (const, eq) with the scalar types int, real, or bool as defined in AGREE.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Assume and guarantee clauses must use only visible ports or locally declared eq/const helpers.
Never introduce package-qualified names without matching 'with' clauses in the base AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not add new subcomponent references or rewrite AADL scopes to resolve AGREE type errors.
Avoid using 'integer' or 'boolean' — use 'int' and 'bool' exclusively in AGREE declarations.
